volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 2.2.457d - Section 2.2.518c)

sections.label: Section 2.2.457d
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.458
sections.translation: By heavens ! ’ twas ill done .

sections.label: Section 2.2.458b
sections.translation: What is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.458c
sections.translation: It cannot be expressed , how shocking and dreadful a mischief you ’ ve been guilty of .

sections.label: Section 2.2.460
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.460b
sections.original: Fuge , obsecro , atque abscede ab aedibus .
sections.translation: Take to flight , I beseech you , and get away from the house . Fly in this direction , fly closer to me . ( He runs to wards TRANIO .) What , did you touch the door ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.462
sections.original: Quo modo pultare potui , si non tangerem ?
sections.translation: How could I knock , if I didn ’ t touch it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.463
sections.translation: By all that ’ s holy , you ’ ve been the death —

sections.label: Section 2.2.463b
sections.translation: Of what person ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.463c
sections.translation: Of all your family .

sections.label: Section 2.2.464
sections.original: Di te deaeque ómnes faxint cum istoc omine —
sections.translation: May the Gods and Goddesses confound you with that omen .

sections.label: Section 2.2.465
sections.original: Metuo , te atque istos expiare ut possies .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid that you can ’ t make satisfaction for yourself and them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.466
sections.original: Quam ob rem ? aut quam subito rem mihi adportas novam ?
sections.translation: For what reason , or what new affair is this that you thus suddenly bring me news of ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.467
sections.original: Et heus , iube illos illinc ambo abscedere .
sections.translation: And ( whispering ) hark you , prithee , do bid those people to move away from here .

sections.label: Section 2.2.468
sections.translation: Move away from here .

sections.label: Section 2.2.468b
sections.translation: Don ’ t you touch the house . Touch you the ground [*] as well . ( Exeunt the ATTENDANTS .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.469b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , prithee , do speak out now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.470
sections.original: Quia septem menses sunt , quom in hasce aedis pedem
sections.translation: Because it is now seven months that not a person has set foot within this house , and since we once for all left it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.472
sections.translation: Tell me , why so ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.472b
sections.translation: Just look around , whether there ’ s any person to overhear our discourse .

sections.label: Section 2.2.473b
sections.translation: All ’ s quite safe .

sections.label: Section 2.2.474
sections.translation: Look around once more .

sections.label: Section 2.2.474b
sections.translation: There ’ s nobody ; now then , speak out .

sections.label: Section 2.2.475
sections.translation: The house has been guilty of a capital offence [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.475b
sections.translation: I don ’ t understand you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.476
sections.original: Scelus , inquam , factum est iam diu , antiquom et vetus .
sections.translation: A crime , I tell you , has been committed there , a long while ago , one of olden time and ancient date .

sections.label: Section 2.2.477
sections.translation: Of ancient date ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.477b
sections.original: Id adeo nos nunc factum invenimus .
sections.translation: ’ Tis but recently , in fact , that we ’ ve discovered this deed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.478
sections.original: Quid istúc est sceleris ? aut quis id fecit ? cedo .
sections.translation: What is this crime , or who committed it ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.479
sections.translation: A host slew his guest , seized with his hand ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.480
sections.original: iste , ut ego opinor , qui has tibi aedis vendidit .
sections.translation: he , I fancy , who sold you the house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.481
sections.translation: Slew him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.481b
sections.translation: And robbed this guest of his gold , and buried this guest there in the house , on the spot .

sections.label: Section 2.2.483
sections.translation: For what reason do you suspect that this took place ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.484
sections.original: Ego dicam , ausculta . út foris cenaverat
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; listen . One day , when your son had dined away from home , after he returned home from dining ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.486
sections.translation: we all went to bed , and fell asleep . By accident , I had forgotten to put out my lamp ; and he , all of a sudden , called out aloud —

sections.label: Section 2.2.489
sections.translation: What person ? My son ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.489b
sections.translation: Hist ! hold your peace : just listen .

sections.label: Section 2.2.490
sections.original: ait vénisse illum in somnis ad se mortuom .
sections.translation: He said that a dead man came to him in his sleep —

sections.label: Section 2.2.491
sections.translation: In his dreams , then , you mean ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.491b
sections.translation: Just so . But only listen . He said that he had met with his death by these means —

sections.label: Section 2.2.493
sections.translation: What , in his sleep ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.493b
sections.translation: It would have been surprising if he had told him awake , who had been murdered sixty years ago .

sections.label: Section 2.2.495
sections.translation: On some occasions you are absurdly simple .

sections.label: Section 2.2.496b
sections.translation: But look , what he said : I am the guest of Diapontius , from beyond the seas ; here do I dwell ; this has been assigned me as my abode ; for Orcus would not receive me in Acheron ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.500
sections.original: quia praemature vita careo . per fidem
sections.translation: because prematurely I lost my life . Through confiding was I deceived : my entertainer slew me here , and that villain secretly laid me in the ground without funereal rites , in this house , on the spot , for the sake of gold . Now do you depart from here ; this house is accursed , this dwelling is defiled .

sections.label: Section 2.2.505
sections.original: quae hic monstra fiunt , anno vix possum eloqui .
sections.translation: The wonders that here take place , hardly in a year could I recount them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.506
sections.translation: Hush , hush !

sections.label: Section 2.2.507
sections.translation: Troth now , what has happened , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.507b
sections.translation: The door made a noise .

sections.label: Section 2.2.508
sections.translation: Was it he that was knocking ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.508b
sections.translation: I have not one drop of blood ! Dead men are come to fetch me to Acheron , while alive !

sections.label: Section 2.2.510
sections.original: Perii , illisce hodie hanc conturbabunt fabulam .
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! those people there will mar my plot . ( A noise is heard from within .) How much I dread , lest he should catch me in the fact .

sections.label: Section 2.2.512
sections.translation: What are you talking about to yourself ? ( Goes near the door .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.512b
sections.translation: Do get away from the door . By heavens , fly , I do beseech you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.513b
sections.translation: Fly where ? Fly yourself , as well .

sections.label: Section 2.2.514
sections.original: Nihil ego formido , pax mihi est cum mortuis .
sections.translation: I am not afraid : I am at peace with the dead .

sections.label: Section 2.2.515
sections.translation: Hallo ! Tranio [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.515b
sections.translation: You won ’ t be calling me , if you are wise . ( Aloud , as if speaking to the APPARITION .) ’ Tis not I that ’ s guilty ; I did not knock at the door .

sections.label: Section 2.2.518c
sections.original: Quae r es te agitat , Tranio ?
sections.translation: Pray , what is it that ’ s wrong ? What matter is agitating you , Tranio ? To whom are you saying these things ?
